oss.prompt: Use the open-source project {name} ({url}) to help me with: [describe your task here]. If {name} is already installed locally, use it directly; otherwise install it from {url} first, then run the task.
oss.install_prompt: Please install the open-source project {name} ({url}) for me. Once it's installed, briefly tell me what it can do.
new_chat.quick.agent_required: Install or enable “{name}” before using this shortcut.
new_chat.quick.tmpl.education: [topic / problem / material] Tutor me step by step: find where I am stuck, explain with examples, and give me a practice plan
new_chat.quick.tmpl.ecommerce: Research [product / category]: analyze demand, competitors, price bands, profit assumptions, sourcing risk, and whether it is worth selling
new_chat.quick.tmpl.rnd: Build [app / feature]: clarify requirements, design the implementation, write code, test it, and verify completion
new_chat.quick.tmpl.creation: Write [topic]: propose the structure and angle, draft it, then polish it for the target audience
new_chat.quick.tmpl.data: Deep research [topic]: gather recent sources, compare evidence by key dimensions, cite links, and produce a structured report
new_chat.quick.tmpl.office: Organize [materials / files]: extract key points and turn them into a polished document, table, presentation, or PDF-ready deliverable

skills.help_finish_seed_model: Please finish this skill. First read SKILL.md, _meta.json, and the other imported files in the current skill directory. If SKILL.md is already complete or this import already installed source files, preserve the body, do not re-emit existing files, and only add necessary skill metadata. If SKILL.md is a placeholder or missing its body, complete it from the imported materials. If the imported content clearly contains multiple existing skills, restore them as separate skills and do not merge them into one; if those skills are already installed, update each one with metadata-only <skill> containers. Keep SKILL.md frontmatter to name and description only; use _meta.json for category, routing.negative_examples, routing.applicable_domain, routing.prerequisites, and other extensions. Set an appropriate category when you can infer one, otherwise use general. Complete anything that can be inferred from the existing content without asking the user. When emitting write protocol, do not add explanatory prose around it; if you need one short user-facing result, use <skill-reply>...</skill-reply>.
skills.clear_confirm: Clear the edit chat for this skill? Files stay untouched.

If missing details can be inferred from the name and summary, proceed without asking the user; do not show internal config blocks or write protocols in the conversation.

settings.localexec.title: Tool Execution Access
settings.localexec.sub: Controls whether agents can run terminal commands, create or edit workspace files, and generate PDFs, images, and interactive apps. Read-only tools are always available.
settings.localexec.mode.off_desc: Agents can only read and analyze files. They cannot run commands or modify files.
settings.localexec.mode.risk_prompt_desc: Agents can run commands and modify files. They ask for your approval before risky actions such as going online, deleting files, or escalating privileges.
settings.localexec.mode.allow_all_desc: Agents run any action without asking. Use this only when you fully trust the current task.
settings.metacognition.title: Metacognition-level Agent Self-Evolution
settings.metacognition.sub: When on, each agent periodically reviews its recent performance, draws lessons, and adjusts how it works so it does better next time. We call it "meta-cognitive" because the agent doesn't just do tasks — it also reasons about "how am I doing, and how could I do better next time?" That cognition about its own cognition is metacognition. Turn off to stop the self-review.
settings.data_root.title: Data directory
settings.data_root.sub: All your data lives under this directory. Click to open it in the file manager.

marketplace.title: Marketplace
marketplace.oss_hero_sub: Hand-picked open-source AI projects — Orkas drives them via install / CLI / MCP in one click.
quality.fix.no_credential_path_read: Do not access credential files directly. Ask the user to provide the path or secret explicitly through an input, parameter, or setting.
quality.fix.no_eval_with_external_input: Avoid eval / exec on non-literal input. Restructure the code to call specific functions or commands explicitly.
quality.fix.no_download_then_execute: Do not pipe remote downloads directly into a shell. Dependencies should be installed by the user through normal package managers.
quality.fix.no_shell_init_or_persistence: Do not modify shell startup files or install persistence services. Skill code must not alter the user's environment.
quality.fix.no_cross_agent_private_read: Do not read other agents' memory, metacognition data, or other skills' SKILL.md files. Each skill or agent should only access its own data.
quality.fix.no_obfuscated_payload: Do not decode and execute hidden payloads. Executable logic should be written in clear text so it can be reviewed.
quality.fix.no_shell_history_read: Do not read shell history files; they often contain temporary credentials and private user context.
quality.fix.no_spec_self_modification: Skill or agent code must not modify its own spec or another skill's spec. Spec changes should go through the editor or the spec_patch_suggester flow.
quality.fix.no_write_outside_workspace: Do not write to system directories. Skills may only write inside the workspace, the system temp directory, or a path explicitly provided by the user.
quality.fix.skill_md_missing: Every skill directory must contain a SKILL.md file.
quality.fix.agent_json_missing: Agent directories must contain an agent.json spec file.
quality.fix.frontmatter_name_missing: Add a `name:` field in the SKILL.md frontmatter.
quality.fix.frontmatter_name_invalid: Skill names should start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, `_`, and `-`; spaces are not allowed.
quality.fix.agent_name_invalid: Agent names should contain only letters, digits, `_`, `-`, or CJK characters; spaces are not allowed.
quality.fix.frontmatter_description_miss: Add a concise `description:` in SKILL.md, or localized descriptions in `_meta.json.descriptions`.
quality.fix.frontmatter_description_too_: Shorten the description; long descriptions are truncated in dispatcher prompts and lose matching signal.
quality.fix.frontmatter_category_missing: Add `category:` using the category codes defined by skill-creator.
quality.fix.frontmatter_category_invalid: Use a safe marketplace category code from skill-creator.
quality.fix.agent_name_missing: Agent specs must include a non-empty `name`.
quality.fix.agent_description_missing: Add `description_zh` and `description_en` so the dispatcher can route to this agent in either UI language.
quality.fix.agent_category_missing: Add `category` using the category codes defined by agent-creator.
quality.fix.agent_category_invalid: Use a safe marketplace category code from agent-creator.
quality.fix.frontmatter_unparseable: SKILL.md frontmatter must be a valid YAML scalar map between two `---` lines.
